title: buildah bud issue
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. buildah bud -f Dockerfile -t xxxx:latest .


**Describe the results you received:**
STEP 1: FROM golang:1.10-alpine
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:4fe2ade4980c2dda4fc95858ebb981489baec8c1e4bd282ab1c3560be8ff9bde

 0 B / 2.10 MiB 
 42.70 KiB / 2.10 MiB 
 126.76 KiB / 2.10 MiB 
 286.76 KiB / 2.10 MiB 
 670.76 KiB / 2.10 MiB 
 1.50 MiB / 2.10 MiB 
 2.10 MiB / 2.10 MiB  1s
Copying blob sha256:2e793f0ebe8a0d4496ef2fca6d5d45493df6dc40503657b357e153fa91548171
STEP 2: MAINTAINER xxx <xxx@xxx.com>
STEP 3: WORKDIR /go/src/component-docker
STEP 4: COPY ./ /go/src/component-docker
panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference
[signal SIGSEGV: segmentation violation code=0x1 addr=0xc0 pc=0xa2b835]

**Describe the results you expected:**
i guess this, 'from' is not success, so 'copy' get error, maybe this is a network question resulting in ‘from’ error. should ‘from’ return a error, not 'copy' return a error

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**
buildah version 1.3 (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)

comments.body: Thanks for the issue report @coderwangke .  This maybe the same as #1105, but we'll take a look.
@coderwangke Why do you think the from was unsuccessful?

@coderwangke Is this repeatable, and could you do this with a --debug flag set on the bud command.

buildah --debug bud ...

@coderwangke I gave this a go with the latest from GitHub and it seemed to work for me.  I ran as root and ran it from a subdirectory rather than the top level directory.  Can you tell me if you ran it as root and from what directory?  Is my Dockerfile and commands below OK?  If you can, please upgrade and retest, it may be something that was fixed in v1.4.  Also as @rhatdan asked, a run with --debug would be helpful too.

```
# buildah version
Version:         1.5-dev
Go Version:      go1.9.7
Image Spec:      1.0.0
Runtime Spec:    1.0.0
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  v0.7.0-alpha1
Git Commit:      52d797d6
Built:           Tue Oct 16 19:56:07 2018
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64

# cat Dockerfile
FROM golang:1.10-alpine
MAINTAINER tom tsweeney@redhat.com
WORKDIR /go/src/component-docker
COPY ./ /go/src/component-docker

# buildah bud -f Dockerfile -t tom:latest .
STEP 1: FROM golang:1.10-alpine
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:4fe2ade4980c2dda4fc95858ebb981489baec8c1e4bd282ab1c3560be8ff9bde

Copying config sha256:718b456c4b757cd80f6353b51081792b3942e008a169df29fe3d388c7c3d8554

Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> 718b456c4b757cd80f6353b51081792b3942e008a169df29fe3d388c7c3d8554

```

@rhatdan @TomSweeneyRedHat 
I further tested and verified my guess by running with --debug. When there is a problem with from, it does not return, but when the copy is executed, the error is returned. The reason for the failure of from is due to the network speed.
#### Limit network speed
sudo wondershaper eth0 5 5

#### buildah --debug bud -f /root/src/buildah-test-dockerfile/Dockerfile -t test:latest /root/src/buildah-test-dockerfile/
